mace
Provides:
No public official at any level of state or local
government shall make, participate in making or in any
way attempt to use his oéficial position to influence a
governmental decision in which he Knows or has reason
to know he has a financial interesci
Mr. Matthews is within the scope of this mandate, for he is
a memher of a local government agency possessing dccisiom
mating authority.
12 cal. Adm. code section 18700(a)(1).)
A definltion of the term “financial Interest" is provided
by section 87103:
An official has a financial interest in a decision
within the meaning of Section H7100 it it is reasonably
toreseeahle that the decision Will have a material
tinancial effect, distinguishable from its effect on
the puhlic generally, en;
la)

has a direct or indirect investucnt north mart. than one
thousand dollars (31,000).
(b)
Any real property in which the public niticial
has a direct or indirect interest worth more than one
thousand dollars {SLUDOL
(c)
Any source ot income, other than loans by a
commercial lending institution in the regular course of
business on terms available to the public without regard
3/

unless otherwise noted,

In answer to the first question you presented, the
analysis focuses on whether, as a memher of the interim
Design Review Committee, Mr. Matthews will be making decisions,
regarding the Pulliam, Matthews
t. Associates' building, that
will have a material effect on his financial interests.
"Material financial effect" is explained in 2 Cal. Adm. Code
section lsmz.
(Please refer to encloscd copy.l
Decisions afiectinq the cost of constructing the building
could have a material financial effect on the current assets
or liahilitles of Pullian, Matthews
i Associates sufficient

2 Cal.
Adm.
code Section la7nzrbl<ll<chl
Also, datismns regarding
its construction may materially affect Mr. Matthews- ownership
interest in the building by increasing or decreasing tnc
fair market value of it by at least $1,000,
[See 2 cal.
Adm. Code Section lB7llzrb7r2HB).‘,
Mr. Mattnizwﬁis required,
therefore,
to disqualify himself tron participating in such
decisions.
it must be noted, however, that while he will
probably have to abstain from voting on most of these matters,
the Political Reform Act does not regulre disqualification
where there is no material financial effect, such as a vote
on whether to require the installation of a ricycie racx.


‘
Victor J. Kaleta
November 9, 1981
rage Four
your second guestion concerns whether nr. Matthews will
be making decisions, as to construction in the area immediately
surrounding the pulliam, Matthews
5. Associates~ huilning,
which will have a material financial effect on his interests.
Certain decisions could well have. such an impact and require
disqualificationi
An example of this would be
a decision to
allow an industrial park to locate in the neighborhood.
This could could have a strong effect on the gross and net
revenues Mr. Matthews receives from pulliam, Matthews
t
Associates because it could generate business for the iirm.
Also.
a decision, as to whether to allow a skyscraper to he
constructed, could increase or decrease the fair market
value of the Pulliam, Matthews a Associates' building hy
$1,000.
on the other hand, there may be many decisions in which
Mr. Matthews could participate.
For example, the impact on
the Pulliam, Matthews and Associates' building, of a decision
regarding the color of paint to be used on the surrounding
buildings would appear to be minimal.
h case—hy—case determination
must be madei
Finally, your last question is whether Mr. Matthews
would have a conflict of interest in making decisions which
affect other areas located within the interim Design Review
Distrlct,
The potential for a financial effect resulting
from these decisions is much more attnnuated thin in previous
questions.
Although, arguably, all decisions made in the
District have some effect on the area as
a whole, the chance
that these will have a material financial effect on Mr. Matthews-
interests appears slim.
There are some decisions, though,
which could have such an effect, an example being the one
cited above, involving the construction of an industrial
park,
Such a development could generate greater revenues
for Pulliam, Matthews
a Associates, even if not located in
the immediate neighborhood.
it is important, though, that
each such decis;on be scrutinized for a potential conflict
of interest in light of its specific facts.
ns this letter has demonstrated,
the political Reform
Act does not require Mr. Matthews to resign from the lnterim
Design Review committee, but rather,
it reguircs him to be
aware of potential conflicts of interest and to disqualify
himself when necessary.
Thank you for consulting us on this

Dear Ms.
Milmall:
This is e request for written advice for Mortimer Jr Matthews,
pursuant
to Government Code Section
H3llA(b)
and
2 Cal.
Admin.
Code
Section
l8329.
Factual
Background
Mr.
Matthews,
an architect;
is
the Vice—Chairman of
the
City's
cultural Heritage commission and serves as its representative
on the Interim Design Review Committee established by
Ordinance No.
5091,
a copy of which is attached heretor The
Interim Design Review Committee reviews
all new construction
and exterior alterations
within
the
Interim Design
Review
District within
the Central
Business
District.
The
Interim
Design
Review District
is
defined
as
that
area
from Pasadena
Avenue
to Wilson Avenue,
210
Freeway
to California,
exclusive
of Old Pasadenal
Ordinance No.
5491 contains
a map of
the
District.
Additionally,
I have enclosed
a marked aerial
photograph of the District, a marked map with potential
development, and a marked street map.
Mrl
Matthews
is
an officer and a
50
percent shareholder
in
Pullxazn,
Matthews
Sr Associates
which has
been
selected
to
develop a parcel of city property at the northwest corner
of walnut Street and Marengo Avenue,
a property within
the
Interim Design Review District.
The
location
is shown on
the


Page
2 — Barbara Milman
catcher 14, 1981
marked street map.
This office has opined that ”L Matthews is disqualified
from reviewing the design of the Pulliam, Matthews
3.
Associates building and any construction within close
proximity to said building as shown on the marked street
map.
A5 to the balance of the Interim Design Review
District, this office could not foresee a financial
interest but recommended that Mri Matthews resign from
the Interim Design Review Committee to avoid potential
conflict of interest charges.
Questions Posed
The specific questions Mr. Matthews requires written advice
on are as follows:
i.
Does Mr. Matthews have a (inanclal interest in
decisions of the interim Design aeView
Committee as concerns the design of the Pulliam,
Matthews & Associates building?
2.
Does Mz. Matthews have a financial intorost in
such decisions as concerns construction and
alterations within close proximity to said
building as shown on the marked street map?
3.
Does Mr. Matthews have a financial interest
in such decisions as concerns the balance of
the Interim Design Review Distrlct?
If you require further (actual material to answer the above
questions. please contact the undersigned
Very truly yours,
Evelynn M. Finn
city attorney
Victor J. Kaleta
Deputy city Attorney

sections.question:
s.
Although, arguably, all decisions made in the
District have some effect on the area as
a whole, the chance
that these will have a material financial effect on Mr. Matthews-
interests appears slim.
There are some decisions, though,
which could have such an effect, an example being the one
cited above, involving the construction of an industrial
park,
Such a development could generate greater revenues
for Pulliam, Matthews
a Associates, even if not located in
the immediate neighborhood.
it is important, though, that
each such decis;on be scrutinized for a potential conflict
of interest in light of its specific facts.
ns this letter has demonstrated,
the political Reform
Act does not require Mr. Matthews to resign from the lnterim
Design Review committee, but rather,
it reguircs him to be
aware of potential conflicts of interest and to disqualify
himself when necessary.
Thank you for consulting us on this


i
‘
Victor J. Kaleza
November 9, 1991
Page Five
issue.
If you have any add1t1onal qucstlons, plaase feel
free to Contact me at (915) 322—5901.
sections.conclusion: The political Reform Act does not require Mr. Matthews to resign from the Interim Design Review Committee, but rather, it requires him to be aware of potential conflicts of interest and to disqualify himself when necessary. Mr. Matthews is required to disqualify himself from participating in decisions affecting the cost and fair market value of the Pulliam, Matthews & Associates building. For decisions regarding construction in the immediate vicinity, case-by-case determination is necessary based on material financial effects. For decisions affecting other areas of the District, the potential for material financial effect is attenuated, though some decisions (like industrial park development) could require disqualification.

sections.question_synthetic: Whether Mortimer Matthews, an officer and 50% shareholder in an architectural firm selected to develop city property within an Interim Design Review District, has financial conflicts of interest that would require him to disqualify himself from committee decisions regarding his firm's building, nearby construction, and the broader district.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Mr. Matthews must disqualify himself from decisions directly affecting his building's construction costs and fair market value, must evaluate case-by-case decisions regarding proximate construction, and generally may participate in decisions affecting the broader district unless they would materially affect his financial interests.
sections.parsing_notes: Mixed formats: ocr, old; LLM: Document contains both incoming request letter (pages with Victor Kaleta's incoming questions) and FPPC response letter (James L. Shank's advice). OCR quality issues present but content substantially legible. Question and conclusion sections clearly identifiable. Date in signature shows November 9, 1991 but header indicates 1981; 1981 is correct based on document ID 81A506 and letter content dates.

QUESTION: s.
Although, arguably, all decisions made in the
District have some effect on the area as
a whole, the chance
that these will have a material financial effect on Mr. Matthews-
interests appears slim.
There are some decisions, though,
which could have such an effect, an example being the one
cited above, involving the construction of an industrial
park,
Such a development could generate greater revenues
for Pulliam, Matthews
a Associates, even if not located in
the immediate neighborhood.
it is important, though, that
each such decis;on be scrutinized for a potential conflict
of interest in light of its specific facts.
ns this letter has demonstrated,
the political Reform
Act does not require Mr. Matthews to resign from the lnterim
Design Review committee, but rather,
it reguircs him to be
aware of potential conflicts of interest and to disqualify
himself when necessary.
Thank you for consulting us on this


i
‘
Victor J. Kaleza
November 9, 1991
Page Five
issue.
If you have any add1t1onal qucstlons, plaase feel
free to Contact me at (915) 322—5901.

CONCLUSION: The political Reform Act does not require Mr. Matthews to resign from the Interim Design Review Committee, but rather, it requires him to be aware of potential conflicts of interest and to disqualify himself when necessary. Mr. Matthews is required to disqualify himself from participating in decisions affecting the cost and fair market value of the Pulliam, Matthews & Associates building. For decisions regarding construction in the immediate vicinity, case-by-case determination is necessary based on material financial effects. For decisions affecting other areas of the District, the potential for material financial effect is attenuated, though some decisions (like industrial park development) could require disqualification.

embedding.summary: The FPPC advises that Mortimer Matthews, an architect and officer/50% shareholder in Pulliam, Matthews & Associates, has financial interests requiring disqualification under Government Code Section 87100 from certain decisions of the Interim Design Review Committee, but does not need to resign, only to disqualify himself on a case-by-case basis where material financial effects exist.
